name: Haptic Feedback Architecture — iOS Core Haptics + Android
definition_origin: Apple's Taptic Engine debuted in Apple Watch (2015) and iPhone 6s (2015); the Core Haptics framework with the AHAP (Apple Haptic Audio Pattern) JSON file format was introduced at WWDC 2019 (session 520, 'Introducing Core Haptics'). Android added richer haptics via VibrationEffect (API 26, 2017) and VibrationEffect.Composition primitives (API 30, 2020). HIG 'Playing Haptics' page formalizes the design vocabulary. Foundational research is Lynette Jones' 'Haptics' (MIT Press, 2018) and the IEEE Transactions on Haptics body of work.
core_mechanism: Haptics work because the somatosensory cortex processes vibrotactile signals on a parallel channel from vision and audio — feedback registered through skin reaches awareness ~30-40ms faster than visual confirmation, and pre-attentively (no eye-on-screen required). A short, sharp haptic on a button press collapses uncertainty about whether the touch registered, reducing the milliseconds-of-doubt that produce double-taps. Continuous-intensity haptics (like a Taptic Engine ramp) can simulate the inertial feel of physical objects (the iOS Date Picker's notch detent), tying digital interaction to embodied-cognition expectations. When paired with audio (audio-haptic synchronization), the combined cross-modal signal exceeds the perceptual sum of either alone — Apple's 'Practice Audio Haptic Design' WWDC21 framing.
neuroscience_correlate: Primary somatosensory cortex (S1) and secondary somatosensory cortex (S2) for tactile signal processing. Pacinian corpuscles in fingertip skin tuned to 200-300Hz vibrotactile range — the Taptic Engine's resonant frequency (~230Hz) is engineered to this. Cross-modal integration in the superior temporal sulcus binds audio + haptic into a single perceived event. Mesolimbic dopamine spikes on satisfying haptic-confirmed completions (the 'click' of unlocking).
empirical_evidence: Apple's WWDC19 Core Haptics talk reported user-confidence gains in tactile-confirmed UI vs. visual-only baselines [exact figures not public]. IEEE Transactions on Haptics studies (e.g., Choi & Kuchenbecker 2013) show vibrotactile feedback reduces error rates 15-30% on touchscreen typing. Google's Material haptics research (2022) found composed primitives improved task-completion satisfaction ~12-18% over basic vibration on Pixel 6+ devices [uncertain — exact numbers from Android Open Source Project docs]. A widely-cited Boréas/UserTesting industry study found that disabling iOS 16 keyboard haptics increased reported battery life by ~3-7% — confirming the real but small power cost.
measurable_outcome_metric: Touch-error rate (lower); time-to-task-complete on touchpath flows; user-reported 'satisfying' rating; haptic-on retention setting (% of users who keep haptics enabled is a proxy for non-annoying tuning); battery-impact regression test (haptic events per session × measured mAh delta).
engineering_tactics: iOS — three layers, choose the right one: (1) UIFeedbackGenerator family (UIKit, iOS 10+) for predefined patterns: UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(.light/.medium/.heavy/.rigid/.soft), UISelectionFeedbackGenerator (for picker scroll), UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success/.warning/.error). Always prepare() before the trigger to wake the Taptic Engine (otherwise first haptic lags ~150ms). (2) SwiftUI sensoryFeedback (iOS 17+, native): .sensoryFeedback(.success, trigger: completedCount) — automatically respects accessibility settings. (3) Core Haptics CHHapticEngine + AHAP for custom patterns — load from .ahap JSON file: let url = Bundle.main.url(forResource: 'celebration', withExtension: 'ahap'); try engine.playPattern(from: url). MINIMAL AHAP example: { "Version": 1.0, "Pattern": [ { "Event": { "Time": 0.0, "EventType": "HapticTransient", "EventParameters": [ {"ParameterID": "HapticIntensity", "ParameterValue": 1.0}, {"ParameterID": "HapticSharpness", "ParameterValue": 0.7} ] } }, { "Event": { "Time": 0.15, "EventType": "HapticContinuous", "EventDuration": 0.3, "EventParameters": [ {"ParameterID": "HapticIntensity", "ParameterValue": 0.6}, {"ParameterID": "HapticSharpness", "ParameterValue": 0.4} ] } } ] }. Always set CHHapticEngine.resetHandler and stoppedHandler — the engine can be killed by audio-session interruptions. Android — three layers: (1) View.performHapticFeedback(HapticFeedbackConstants.CONFIRM / REJECT / GESTURE_START / etc.) — no permission needed, fallbacks built in. (2) VibrationEffect.createOneShot(50, VibrationEffect.DEFAULT_AMPLITUDE) for simple, createWaveform(timings, amplitudes, repeat) for patterns, createPredefined(EFFECT_CLICK / EFFECT_DOUBLE_CLICK / EFFECT_HEAVY_CLICK / EFFECT_TICK) for tuned device-specific effects (API 29+). (3) VibrationEffect.startComposition().addPrimitive(PRIMITIVE_CLICK, 1.0f, 0).addPrimitive(PRIMITIVE_TICK, 0.5f, 80).compose() — API 30+, but check Vibrator.areAllPrimitivesSupported() first because Android does NOT auto-fallback. CROSS-PLATFORM (React Native): import * as Haptics from 'expo-haptics'; Haptics.selectionAsync() / Haptics.impactAsync(Haptics.ImpactFeedbackStyle.Medium) / Haptics.notificationAsync(Haptics.NotificationFeedbackType.Success). Wrap in a single useHaptic() hook with feature flags so users can disable globally. PAIRING: every notificationAsync should be paired with a sub-300ms visual cue and (optionally) a synced audio asset for audio-haptic alignment.
real_world_examples: Apple Pay double-press → ApplePay open: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator success on confirm — single best-known haptic pattern in iOS. iOS 16+ keyboard typing haptic — controlled in Settings > Sounds & Haptics > Keyboard Feedback (off by default because of battery cost). Apple Stocks scrub timeline: continuous CHHapticEngine tick per data point as finger drags — pure Core Haptics. Robinhood candle-chart scrub: 120fps haptic ticks synced with line redraw, custom AHAP. iOS Camera shutter: synthetic shutter sound + sharp transient haptic — feels mechanical despite being entirely simulated. Duolingo lesson-correct: Haptics.notificationAsync(Success) pre-prepared and triggered ~50ms before checkmark animation, audio sting layered on top. Headspace meditation breathing exercise: continuous low-intensity (0.2) HapticContinuous to guide inhale/exhale rhythm — ambient haptic. Twitter/X like-tap: UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(.light) on tap, then heavier (.medium) on confirm — two-stage haptic feedback. Things 3 task-complete swipe: synced cubic-bezier visual + UISelectionFeedbackGenerator tick — small but iconic.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Massive difference. Web has only the Vibration API (navigator.vibrate(50)) which is on/off pulse — no intensity, no sharpness, no composition; not supported in Safari at all. Web is essentially haptic-free. Mobile is the entire surface area; iPhones (Taptic Engine) and modern Pixels/flagship Samsungs offer rich, composed, intensity-variable haptics. iPad does NOT have a Taptic Engine — guard with code (iOS) and design fallbacks. Apple Watch's Taptic Engine is the most refined and supports custom CHHapticEngine patterns since watchOS 6.
platform_design_language_alignment: Both iOS and Android 2026 platform languages assume haptics. iOS 26 Liquid Glass animations are tuned to pair with sensoryFeedback by default, and Apple's WWDC 2024+ talks treat haptic-visual sync as table stakes. Material 3 Expressive (Android 16+) ships HapticFeedbackConstants tied to Compose modifiers (Modifier.haptic). Build with this assumption — ship-faster guidance is to use the platform's predefined feedback first and only build custom AHAP/Composition for signature brand moments.
claude_design_application: Always specify trigger, type, intensity, and accessibility guard. Example prompt: 'Add haptic feedback to the habit-completion flow in React Native using expo-haptics. On tap of complete button, immediately call Haptics.selectionAsync(). On submission success (after server returns), call Haptics.notificationAsync(Haptics.NotificationFeedbackType.Success). Wrap both in a useHaptic() hook that reads a global setting from Zustand (settings.hapticsEnabled). Pre-warm with Haptics.selectionAsync() on screen mount if the user hasn\u0027t felt one yet, to avoid first-tap lag. Forbid impactAsync on every minor button — only on milestone events.' For iOS native: 'Use SwiftUI .sensoryFeedback(.success, trigger: completedCount) — do not use UINotificationFeedbackGenerator directly. For the streak-celebration custom pattern, load celebration.ahap from bundle and play via CHHapticEngine.'
stitch_application: Stitch CANNOT simulate haptics — they are imperceptible in static visual mockups. Pivot to describing the visual moment the haptic accompanies, in elaborate prose, AND label haptic intent so devs implement it. Example fragment: 'When the user completes their habit and taps Mark Done, the button collapses 4% in scale over 120ms (cubic-bezier 0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1). At the moment of fullest compression, a soft star burst (3 small particles, gold #F5C842) emits radially 24px and fades over 320ms. SIMULTANEOUS HAPTIC INTENT (not visualized): a single sharp transient (HapticIntensity 0.9, HapticSharpness 0.85) at t=0, followed 120ms later by a softer transient (Intensity 0.4, Sharpness 0.3) — like a confirm-tap followed by an echo. Devs: implement via Haptics.notificationAsync(Success) or custom AHAP. Audio cue (also not visualized): 240Hz pluck, 80ms duration, 60% volume. The whole moment is engineered to feel like locking a tiny mechanism shut.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: (1) AI calls UIImpactFeedbackGenerator on every button — overuse desensitizes and drains battery. Counter: 'Haptics only on milestones (submit, complete, error, milestone). Never on hover, scroll, or simple navigation.' (2) AI uses .heavy intensity by default — feels aggressive. Counter: 'Default to .light or .soft. Use .heavy only on confirmation of irreversible action.' (3) AI omits .prepare() on UIKit feedback generators → first-tap lag of ~150ms. Counter: 'Always prepare() the generator on view/screen mount, then call again before each trigger.' (4) AI sends Android composition primitives without checking Vibrator.areAllPrimitivesSupported() → silent failure on devices that don't support them. Counter: 'Always feature-detect with areAllPrimitivesSupported and fallback to VibrationEffect.createPredefined(EFFECT_CLICK).' (5) AI puts haptics on every Lottie loop tick or animation frame → buzzing nightmare. Counter: 'One haptic per discrete moment. Never bind to animation onUpdate.' (6) AI ignores accessibility — never reads UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled or system-haptics-disabled. Counter: 'Wrap all haptic calls behind a setting that respects system Reduce Motion AND a user-level toggle.' (7) AI omits intensity tuning — uses sledgehammer 1.0 for everything. Counter: 'AHAP HapticIntensity in 0.3-0.8 range for confirmations; reserve 1.0 for failure/error punctuation.'
anti_patterns: Buzzing on every keystroke (only good if user opted in via Settings). Haptic on hover/scroll. Continuous vibration as 'attention' (feels manipulative, drains battery). Haptic with no visual peer — the user can't tell what just happened. Heavy haptic on every notification arrival (rapid-fire desensitization). Long durations >500ms (the engine actually vibrates the phone audibly, leaks privacy in a meeting). Audio-haptic mismatch — sound says ping, haptic feels thunk. AI-slop variant: every onPress wired to Haptics.impactAsync(Heavy).
regulatory_risk: Low-Medium. Excessive vibration on non-notification flows can fall under DSA Art. 25 'manipulative interface design' if used to nudge engagement on streaks/FOMO. Accessibility laws (ADA, EN 301 549, EU Accessibility Act 2025) require equivalent non-haptic feedback path for users who disable haptics or have sensory disabilities. COPPA and UK Age-Appropriate Design Code: do not use haptic punishment patterns (long buzz on failure) on minors' apps. Haptic-only feedback (no visual) violates WCAG 1.3.3 (Sensory Characteristics).
pitfalls_caveats: Battery: ~0.2-3% per heavy session — small but measurable, especially on iOS keyboard haptics. Privacy: audible vibration in quiet rooms. iPad has no Taptic Engine — gracefully degrade. Older Android devices have a single rotating-mass actuator (ERM) that cannot do composition — must feature-detect. Accessibility: must pair with visual cue, must respect system 'Vibration' / 'Haptic Touch' off settings. Performance: spinning up CHHapticEngine takes ~100-200ms — pre-warm on screen mount.
cross_cultural_validity: Vibrotactile perception is biologically universal — Pacinian corpuscle frequency response does not vary by culture. Convention varies: Japanese mobile users historically used silent-mode-with-haptics on commuter trains, making haptic feedback a daily ambient signal; Western users skew toward turning haptics off in meetings. Patterns coded as 'success' (rising intensity) vs. 'error' (sharp double-pulse) generalize cross-culturally. RTL languages do not affect haptic design. No evidence of culturally-specific haptic conventions [unverified for African and South Asian markets].
sources: [Representing Haptic Patterns in AHAP Files — Apple Developer](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/corehaptics/representing-haptic-patterns-in-ahap-files)
